todaysEvents.summary: Israel struck Iran's South Pars gasfield on Mar 19 — direct attack on Iran's largest gas complex, marking a new phase of energy infrastructure warfare and threatening Iran's domestic gas supply. (Mar 19)

Iran retaliated by attacking Qatar's LNG hub at Ras Laffan and a Saudi Red Sea refinery on Mar 19; European gas soared 35%; Saudi Arabia warned of 'military actions, if necessary,' raising the risk of Saudi entry into the conflict. (Mar 19)
todaysEvents.delta: Iran strikes Qatar LNG hub; Saudi warns

todaysEvents.summary: Trump suspended the Jones Act for 60 days and the administration is weighing large additional troop deployments to the region; Arab and Muslim foreign ministers convened in Riyadh in a diplomatic bind over response. (Mar 19)
todaysEvents.delta: Jones Act suspended; troops weighing; Riyadh FM meeting

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Israel–Iran exchange remains confined to defined military and energy infrastructure targets; Saudi Arabia does not enter the conflict. Bilateral Hormuz transit deals persist in degraded form; Qatar LNG hub attack is repaired within weeks. Brent settles $110-125/bbl; JKM spikes but retreats as Australian and US supply fills gaps. Saudi warning is deterrence posturing. No allied coalition forms; US acts alone. P&I cover restoration unlikely without formal ceasefire.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Qatar LNG hub damage is severe; Ras Laffan restart delayed 4-8 weeks. Saudi Arabia initiates limited retaliatory strikes on Iran, pulling GCC states deeper into the conflict. Brent tests $130-145/bbl; JKM grinds to $26+/MMBtu with no Qatar restart path. ASEAN oil-importer FX (IDR, PHP, THB) under acute pressure; iTraxx Asia IG widens toward 185-200bp. Jones Act suspension and SPR draws provide only marginal relief. IEA second release approved but insufficient to close the supply gap.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Saudi Arabia enters the conflict following further Iranian strikes on GCC infrastructure; IRGC activates Strait of Malacca threat. Full commercial Hormuz and Red Sea blockade; Ras Laffan permanently offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. US troop surge triggers counter-escalation; allied nations remain absent. P&I cover unavailable across the region through H2 2026.
